sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.34 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: (I.\,D.\,Macdonald). If the identity$[x,\, y]^n=1$holds on a group, is the derived subgroup of the group periodic? { V.\,D.\,Mazurov} No, not always (G.\,S.\,Deryabina, P.\,A.\,Kozhevnikov, Commun. Algebra, { 27}, no.\,9 (1999), 4525--4530; \ S.\,I.\,Adyan, Dokl. Math., { 62}, no.\,2 (2000), 174--176).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
